info.description: The Omni API enables programmatic access to dashboards, documents, models, and other resources.
info.title: Omni API

tags.description: AI-powered data analysis. Submit natural language questions as synchronous queries or asynchronous jobs, and retrieve results including generated queries, data, and summarized answers.
tags.name: AI

tags.description: Unstable API routes - subject to change
tags.name: Unstable

bearerAuth.description: Include in the Authorization header as: Authorization: Bearer <token>

isSecret.description: Whether the variable value is secret

value.description: Updated variable value. Omit or set to null to keep the existing value for secret variables.

DbtEnvironmentVariableUpdate.description: Update an existing variable by ID. Variable names cannot be changed after creation.
DbtEnvironmentVariableUpdate.title: DbtEnvironmentVariableUpdate

detail.description: Detailed error message explaining why query generation failed.
detail.example: The AI was unable to generate a query for this prompt. Try rephrasing your question to be more specific about the data you want to retrieve.

error.description: Error details if query generation failed. Null on success.

result.description: Query execution results as a JSON object. Only present when runQuery is true (the default) and the query executed successfully. The structure contains the query result data.

topic.description: The topic name that was used for query generation.

workbookUrl.description: URL to view and edit the generated query in an Omni workbook. Only present when workbookUrl was set to true in the request.

fields.description: Ordered list of fully qualified field names to include in the query (e.g., "view_name.field_name").

filters.description: Filter conditions keyed by fully qualified field name. Filter values vary by field type.

limit.description: Maximum number of rows to return.

sorts.description: Sort specifications applied to the query results.

table.description: The base topic or view name for the query.

AiSemanticQuery.description: The generated semantic query definition. Null if generation failed. This query can be passed directly to the POST /api/v1/query/run endpoint.

column_name.description: Fully qualified field name to sort by (e.g., "view_name.field_name").

sort_descending.description: Whether to sort in descending order.

detail.description: Human-readable error message describing what went wrong.

status.description: HTTP status code of the error.

detail.description: Human-readable error message describing what went wrong.
detail.example: Unauthorized: Missing or invalid API key

status.description: HTTP status code of the error.

detail.description: Human-readable error message describing what went wrong.
detail.example: Forbidden: AI query generation is not enabled for this organization

status.description: HTTP status code of the error.

detail.description: Human-readable error message describing what went wrong.

status.description: HTTP status code of the error.

prompt.description: The natural language prompt describing the data you want to retrieve.
prompt.example: Show me total revenue by month for the last year

queryAllViews.description: If true and the model has query_all_views_and_fields enabled, AI can query views not in any topic.

runQuery.description: Whether to execute the generated query and return results. Defaults to true. Set to false to only generate the query definition without executing it.

userId.description: User ID to execute the query as. Their permissions will be applied for row-level security. Only valid with organization-scoped API keys. Personal access tokens always act as the authenticated user.

workbookUrl.description: If true, creates a new workbook with the generated query and returns its URL. Useful for sharing results or further exploration.

branchId.description: Optional branch ID for the model. Must be a branch of the shared model specified by modelId.

currentTopicName.description: The name of the current topic to scope query generation. If not provided, AI will automatically select the best topic for your prompt.

modelId.description: The UUID of the shared model to query against. Only shared models are supported.

topicId.description: The name of the topic that best matches the prompt. Use this as the topicName parameter when calling generate-query or submitting an AI job.

potentialTopicNames.description: Optional list of topic names to limit consideration to. If not provided, all topics the user has access to in the model will be evaluated.

prompt.description: The natural language prompt to analyze. The AI will determine which topic best matches the data described in this prompt.
prompt.example: How many orders were placed last month?

userId.description: User ID to evaluate topic access as. Their permissions will be used for permission-aware topic selection. Only valid with organization-scoped API keys. Personal access tokens always act as the authenticated user.

answer.description: A synthesized answer to the question, based on the Omni documentation.
answer.example: To create a dashboard filter, navigate to your dashboard and click the "Add Filter" button...

title.description: The title of the source documentation page.

url.description: URL of the source documentation page.

sources.description: List of documentation pages that were used to synthesize the answer.

question.description: A natural language question about Omni features, configuration, modeling, dashboards, or other topics covered in the Omni documentation.
question.example: How do I create a dashboard filter?

conversationId.description: The conversation ID for this job. Pass this as conversationId in subsequent job submissions to continue the conversation with additional context.

jobId.description: The unique identifier for the created job. Use this to poll status via GET /api/v1/ai/jobs/{jobId} or retrieve results via GET /api/v1/ai/jobs/{jobId}/result.

omniChatUrl.description: URL to view this conversation in the Omni chat interface. Opens the chat session where the job actions and results are visible.

detail.description: Human-readable error message describing what went wrong.
detail.example: An active job already exists for this conversation

status.description: HTTP status code of the error.

branchId.description: Optional branch ID for the model. Must be a branch of the shared model specified by modelId. Use this to query against in-progress model changes.

conversationId.description: Conversation ID to continue an existing conversation thread. The AI will have access to the context from previous jobs in the same conversation. If omitted, a new conversation is created. Only one active job can exist per conversation.

modelId.description: The UUID of the shared model to query against. Only shared models are supported.

progressWebhookEnabled.description: When true, real-time progress events are POSTed to webhookUrl during execution (e.g., "Searching for revenue fields", "Query returned 42 rows"). Requires webhookUrl. Progress events are best-effort: single attempt, no retries, failures do not affect job execution.

prompt.description: The natural language prompt for the AI to process. The AI will analyze your question, generate appropriate queries, execute them, and return a summarized answer.
prompt.example: What are the top 5 products by revenue this quarter?

topicName.description: Topic name to scope query generation. Topics define a set of related views and their join paths. If not provided, the AI will automatically select the best topic. Use the pick-topic endpoint to determine the right topic programmatically.

webhookMetadata.description: Arbitrary metadata object that will be included unchanged in webhook payloads. Use this to correlate webhook notifications with your own system (e.g., tracking IDs, channel references).

webhookSigningSecret.description: Secret key for HMAC-SHA256 webhook payload signing. When provided, each webhook request includes X-Omni-Signature and X-Omni-Signature-Timestamp headers for verification. Required if webhookUrl is specified.

webhookUrl.description: URL to receive webhook POSTs. Always receives a terminal event (job.complete or job.failed) when the job finishes. When progressWebhookEnabled is true, also receives real-time progress events during execution.

branchId.description: Branch ID used for model context, or null if querying the main shared model.

cancelledAt.description: When the job was cancelled. Only present in CANCELLED state.

cancelledBy.description: User ID of who cancelled the job. Only present in CANCELLED state.

completedAt.description: When the job finished (successfully or with error). Present in COMPLETE and FAILED states.

conversationId.description: The conversation this job belongs to. Use this to submit follow-up jobs in the same conversation thread.

detail.example: The query timed out after 300 seconds

error.description: Error details explaining why the job failed. Only present in FAILED state.

executionStartedAt.description: When execution began. Present once the job transitions from QUEUED to EXECUTING. May be absent on jobs that failed or were cancelled before execution started.

id.description: The unique identifier for this job.

modelId.description: The shared model ID used for query generation.

omniChatUrl.description: URL to view this conversation in the Omni chat interface. Opens the chat session where the job actions and results are visible.

organizationId.description: The organization that owns this job.

iteration.description: Current iteration number. The AI may take multiple iterations to refine queries and generate a complete answer.

message.description: Human-readable status message describing what the AI is currently doing.
message.example: Running query: Top products by revenue

updatedAt.description: When this progress update was recorded.

progress.description: Real-time progress information. Only present in EXECUTING state. Null if no progress has been reported yet. Updated in real-time as the AI works through iterations.

prompt.description: The natural language prompt that was submitted.
prompt.example: What are the top 5 products by revenue?

resultSummary.description: Markdown-formatted summary of the job result. Only present in COMPLETE state. For the full result with query details and data, use GET /api/v1/ai/jobs/{jobId}/result.

state.description: Current state of the job. Terminal states are COMPLETE, FAILED, and CANCELLED. Poll until the job reaches a terminal state.

topicName.description: Topic name used to scope query generation, or null if the AI selected the topic automatically.

updatedAt.description: When the job record was last modified.

userId.description: The user ID who created (or is associated with) this job.

jobId.description: The job ID that was requested to cancel.

state.description: The job state after the cancellation attempt. CANCELLED if the cancellation was successful. If the job was already in a terminal state (COMPLETE, FAILED, CANCELLED), the current state is returned unchanged — the endpoint is idempotent.

actions.description: Ordered list of actions the AI took during execution. Each action represents a step such as generating a query, executing it, or synthesizing a final answer.

message.description: The AI's final response message in Markdown format. This is the complete answer to the original prompt, incorporating data from all executed queries.

omniChatUrl.description: URL to view this conversation in the Omni chat interface. Opens the chat session where the job actions and results are visible.

resultSummary.description: Summary of the job result. Typically matches the final message content.

topic.description: The topic name used for query generation.

message.description: The AI's explanation of what it is doing in this step, written in natural language.
message.example: I'll generate a query to find the top 5 products by total revenue.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this action occurred.

type.description: The type of action. Common types include "generate_query" (query generation and execution) and "summarize" (final answer synthesis).

csvResult.description: Query results formatted as CSV text.
csvResult.example:
Name,Total Revenue
Ray-Ban Sunglasses,"678,994.41"
Levi's 501 Jeans,"475,072.00"

csvResultWasTruncated.description: Whether the CSV data was truncated due to size limits. If true, the full result set may contain additional rows not included in csvResult.

hasResults.description: Whether the query returned any data rows.

query.description: The semantic query definition that was executed. This can be used with the POST /api/v1/query/run endpoint to re-run the query.

queryName.description: Human-readable name describing what this query retrieves.

totalRowCount.description: Total number of rows returned by the query.

AiJobActionQueryResult.description: Query result data. Only present for generate_query action types.

error.description: Human-readable error message describing what went wrong.
error.example: No Arrow IPC data available for visualization

nextCursor.description: Cursor for fetching the next page

totalRecords.description: Total number of records matching the query

isDefaultEnvironment.description: Whether this is the default environment

ownerId.description: User ID of the environment owner, or null if not a personal environment

isSecret.description: Whether the variable value is secret

ownerId.description: User ID of the environment owner. Used to mark development environments belonging to a specific user.

isSecret.description: Whether the variable value is secret

ownerId.description: User ID of the environment owner. Used to mark development environments belonging to a specific user.

variables.description: Environment variables. Variables with an id update existing ones; variables without an id create new ones.

url.description: URL to view the folder in the Omni UI.

lastViewedAt.description: Last time the dashboard was viewed

url.description: URL to view the document. Returns dashboard URL if document has a dashboard, otherwise workbook URL.

job_id.description: ID of the download job. Use this to poll for download status.

enableFormatting.description: Compatible with csv, xlsx & json formats. If true, formatting will be enabled in the output. Note: If true for json format, a queryIdentifierMapKey is required.

expandTablesToShowAllRows.description: Compatible with pdf and png formats. If true, up to 1,000 rows in table visualizations will be included in the delivery. Note: This parameter cannot be used when paperFormat: fit_page.

filterConfig.description: An object specifying the filter conditions to apply to the task. The filter key specified must already exist in the dashboard.

format.description: Output format for the download: pdf, png, csv, xlsx, or json

hideHiddenFields.description: Compatible with csv & xlsx formats. If true, fields marked as hidden won't be displayed in the output.

hideTitle.description: Compatible with pdf & png formats. If true, the content's title will be hidden in the output.

maxRowLimit.description: Compatible with csv, json, & xlsx formats. Used with overrideRowLimit. Specifies the maximum number of rows.

overrideRowLimit.description: Compatible with csv, json, & xlsx formats. If true, the default row limit will be overridden. Note: If true for json and xlsx formats, a queryIdentifierMapKey is required.

paperFormat.description: Compatible with pdf formats. Defines the paper format (size) of the resulting PDF. Must be one of: a3, a4, letter, legal, fit_page, tabloid.

paperOrientation.description: Compatible with pdf formats. Defines the paper orientation of the resulting PDF. Must be one of: portrait, landscape.

queryIdentifierMapKey.description: Required for single tile tasks. The ID of the query to include in a single tile task. Must reference a valid query in the dashboard.

showContentLink.description: Compatible with all formats except link_only. If true, a link to the content will be shown in the output.

showFilters.description: Compatible with all formats except link_only & csv. If true, filters will be shown in the output.

singleColumnLayout.description: Compatible with pdf and png formats. If true, dashboard tiles will be arranged into a single vertical column.

filename.description: Custom filename for the downloaded file (without extension)

controls.description: Control configuration object. Keys are control IDs, values contain controlType, filterId, label, etc.

filterOrder.description: Ordered list of filter IDs defining display order

filters.description: Filter configuration object. Keys are filter IDs, values contain fieldName, viewName, kind, defaultValue, etc.

clearExistingDraft.description: When true, discards any existing draft before applying updates. Required when updating a published document that already has a draft.

controls.description: Partial control updates. Keys are control IDs that must exist in the dashboard.

filterOrder.description: New order for filters. All filter IDs must exist in the dashboard.

filters.description: Partial filter updates. Keys are filter IDs that must exist in the dashboard.

favorites.description: Number of users who favorited this document

_count.description: Document counts (included when _count is in include param)

connectionId.description: Connection ID the document is associated with

hasDashboard.description: Whether the document has an associated dashboard

labels.description: Labels applied to the document (included when labels is in include param)

url.description: URL to view the document. Returns dashboard URL if document has a dashboard, otherwise workbook URL.

branchId.description: Optional branch ID to associate the document with a model branch

facetFilters.description: Enable facet filters on the dashboard

filterOrder.description: Order of filters in the dashboard

metadataVersion.description: Dashboard metadata version (required when metadata is provided)

modelId.description: Shared model ID to base the document on

fields.description: Field names used in the visualization

clearExistingDraft.description: Clear existing draft before updating (for published documents with drafts)
Clear existing draft before updating (for published documents with drafts)

queryIdentifierMapKey.description: Key in the query identifier map

url.description: URL to view this specific query/sheet in the workbook.

queries.description: List of queries in the document

requirePullRequestToPublish.description: Require pull request to publish changes

userGroupIds.description: User group IDs to grant access to

userIds.description: User membership IDs to grant access to

userGroupIds.description: User group IDs to revoke access from

userIds.description: User membership IDs to revoke access from

scope.description: Access scope for the duplicated document

labels.description: Updated list of labels on the document

userId.description: Membership ID of the new owner

principals.description: List of users and groups with access

sessionId.description: Session ID to use for embedding Omni content

externalId.description: External identifier for the user (from your system)

groups.description: Optional list of group names to assign to the user

userAttributes.description: Optional user attributes for row-level security

documents.description: Number of documents in the folder

favorites.description: Number of users who have favorited this folder

ownerId.description: User ID of the folder owner

url.description: URL to view the folder in the Omni UI.

ownerId.description: User ID of the folder owner

name.description: Name of the folder to create

parentFolderId.description: Parent folder ID (omit to create at root level)

userId.description: User ID to create the folder as (for org-scoped API keys only)

success.description: Whether the folder was deleted successfully

path.description: Updated URL path segment for the folder (the folder's own segment only)

name.description: New display name for the folder

path.description: New URL path segment for the folder (alphanumeric and dashes only). This is only the folder's own segment, not the full hierarchical path.

resolvePathConflict.description: When true, automatically resolves path collisions with existing folders by appending a numeric suffix (e.g., my-path-1). When false (default), returns 409 Conflict if the path is already taken. Does not apply to reserved paths, which are always rejected with 400.

accessBoost.description: Whether access boost is enabled for this permit

userGroupId.description: User group ID if this is a group permit

userId.description: User ID if this is a user permit

permits.description: List of permission permits for the folder

success.description: Whether the permissions were added successfully

role.description: Content role to assign (VIEWER, EDITOR, or MANAGER)

userGroupIds.description: User group IDs to grant permission to

userIds.description: User IDs to grant permission to

success.description: Whether the permissions were updated successfully

userGroupIds.description: User group IDs to update permissions for

userIds.description: User IDs to update permissions for

success.description: Whether the permissions were revoked successfully

userGroupIds.description: User group IDs to revoke permissions from

userIds.description: User IDs to revoke permissions from

homepage.description: Whether label is shown on homepage

usage_count.description: Number of documents with this label

homepage.description: Whether label is shown on homepage

usage_count.description: Number of documents with this label

Base view name for the topic

name.description: Sanitized exposure name. May contain duplicates across exposures; use deduplication_name for a guaranteed-unique alternative.

didSync.description: Whether a sync operation was performed

gitSha.description: The git SHA after the sync operation

inSync.description: Whether the model is currently in sync with git

find_or_replace_type.description: Type of find/replace operation. Must be FIELD, VIEW, or TOPIC (REPAIR is not supported via API)

only_in_workbook_id.description: Optional workbook ID to limit the replace scope

fileName.description: File name to create or update

plan.description: Query execution plan (only present if planOnly is true).

slackRecipientType.description: Slack recipient type: Channel or Users (null for non-Slack)

userGroupRecipients.description: List of user group recipients (for email destinations).

post.description: Generate an Omni semantic query from a natural language prompt. Optionally executes the generated query and returns results. The AI analyzes the prompt, selects appropriate fields and filters from the model, and constructs a query. Requires the querier role on the target model.

200.description: Query generated successfully. If runQuery is true (default), includes execution results. Check the error field — a 200 response may still contain a partial error if the query was generated but execution failed.

400.description: Invalid request. The prompt may be missing, the modelId may be invalid, or the AI was unable to generate a query for the given prompt.

403.description: Insufficient permissions. Requires the querier role on the target model and AI query generation must be enabled for the organization.

404.description: The specified model or topic was not found in the organization.

post.description: Analyze a natural language prompt and determine which topic in the model is the best fit for answering the question. Useful as a preprocessing step before calling generate-query or submitting an AI job, especially when the user's question could relate to multiple topics.
post.summary: Pick the best topic for a prompt

200.description: Topic selected successfully. The returned topicId can be used as the topicName parameter in other AI endpoints.

400.description: Invalid request body. The prompt or modelId may be missing or malformed.

403.description: Insufficient permissions. Requires the querier role on the target model and AI must be enabled for the organization.

404.description: The specified model was not found, or no accessible topics exist in the model.

post.description: Search the Omni documentation using AI to answer questions about Omni features, configuration, modeling, dashboards, and more. Sends a natural language question and returns a synthesized answer with source links to the relevant documentation pages.

200.description: Documentation search completed successfully. Returns a synthesized answer with source links.

400.description: Invalid request. The question may be missing or exceed the 2000 character limit.

403.description: Omni Agent is not enabled for this organization.

post.description: Submit a new AI job for asynchronous execution. The AI will analyze the prompt, generate and execute queries against the specified model, and produce a summarized answer. Jobs are processed by a background worker and typically complete within 15–60 seconds. Use GET /api/v1/ai/jobs/{jobId} to poll for status, or configure a webhookUrl to receive a notification when the job completes. Optionally continue an existing conversation by providing a conversationId.

schema.description: Target user membership ID (for org-scoped API keys)

parameters.description: Target user membership ID (for org-scoped API keys)
parameters.name: userId

201.description: Job created and queued for execution. Use the returned jobId to poll for status or retrieve results.

400.description: Invalid request body. Common causes: missing or empty prompt, invalid UUID for modelId/branchId/conversationId, invalid webhook URL format.

403.description: Insufficient permissions. The AI jobs API must be enabled for the organization, AI query generation must be enabled, and the user must have appropriate model access. User-scoped API keys cannot act on behalf of other users.

404.description: The specified model was not found in the organization, the branchId does not belong to the specified model, or the topicName does not exist in the model (or is excluded by ai_chat_topics restrictions).

409.description: An active job already exists for the specified conversationId. Wait for the current job to complete before submitting another job to the same conversation.

get.description: Get the current status of an AI job, including its state, progress information, and result summary. The response fields vary by state — for example, progress is only present during EXECUTING, and resultSummary is only present when COMPLETE. Poll this endpoint every 2–5 seconds until the job reaches a terminal state (COMPLETE, FAILED, or CANCELLED).

schema.description: The unique identifier of the AI job

parameters.description: The unique identifier of the AI job
parameters.name: jobId

200.description: Job status retrieved successfully. Check the state field to determine if the job is still running or has reached a terminal state.

400.description: Invalid job ID format. Must be a valid UUID.

404.description: Job not found. The job may not exist or may belong to a different organization.

post.description: Request cancellation of an AI job. This endpoint is idempotent — calling it on an already-cancelled or completed job returns success with the current state. For QUEUED jobs, cancellation is immediate. For EXECUTING jobs, the worker will stop after completing its current iteration. Jobs in DELIVERING state cannot be cancelled as they are already finalizing results. Only the job owner or organization admins can cancel jobs.

schema.description: The unique identifier of the AI job

parameters.description: The unique identifier of the AI job
parameters.name: jobId

200.description: Cancellation request processed. The state field indicates the job's state after the attempt — CANCELLED if successful, or the current terminal state if the job had already completed.

400.description: Invalid job ID format. Must be a valid UUID.

403.description: Permission denied. Only the job owner or organization admins can cancel jobs.

404.description: Job not found. The job may not exist or may belong to a different organization.

409.description: Concurrent modification conflict. The job state was changed by another request. Retry the cancellation.

get.description: Retrieve the full result of a completed AI job, including all actions taken by the AI (queries generated, data retrieved) and the final summarized answer. Results are only available for jobs in COMPLETE state and are retained for 14 days after completion. The response is streamed directly from storage.

schema.description: The unique identifier of the AI job

parameters.description: The unique identifier of the AI job
parameters.name: jobId

200.description: Full job result including the AI's actions, query results (with CSV data), and the final Markdown-formatted answer.

400.description: Invalid job ID format. Must be a valid UUID.

404.description: Job not found, not in COMPLETE state, or result is no longer available (results are retained for 14 days).

get.description: Render the visualization from a completed AI job as a PNG image. The endpoint extracts the visualization configuration from the job result, loads Arrow IPC data, and renders it server-side using Vega. For style-only follow-ups (e.g., "make it a bar chart"), the endpoint walks back through previous jobs in the conversation to find the original query data.

schema.description: The unique identifier of the AI job

parameters.description: The unique identifier of the AI job
parameters.name: jobId

200.description: Visualization rendered as a PNG image. The Content-Type header is image/png.

400.description: Invalid job ID format. Must be a valid UUID.

404.description: Job not found, not in COMPLETE state, or the apiAiVis feature flag is not enabled.

422.description: The job completed but cannot be rendered as a visualization. Common causes: no visualization action in the job result, no Arrow IPC data available, missing summary fields, or the chart type is not renderable as an image (e.g., tables, KPIs).

bucketName.description: S3 bucket name (S3 destination only). Must be 3-63 characters, lowercase.

fanOut.description: If true, send personalized emails to each recipient (email only)

filterConfig.description: Filter conditions to apply to the task

format.description: The output format: link_only, pdf, png, csv, xlsx, json

hideHiddenFields.description: If true, hidden fields won't be displayed (csv/xlsx only)

hideTitle.description: If true, hide the title in output (pdf/png only)

identifier.description: The ID of the dashboard to schedule

keyPrefix.description: S3 key prefix / folder path (S3 destination only). Leading slashes are normalized.

killJobsOnFailure.description: If true, stop entire job if any queries fail

name.description: The name of the scheduled task

recipients.description: Email recipients (email destination only). For Slack destinations, use the "recipients" field with a channel ID string or user ID(s) as a string or array.

region.description: AWS region where the S3 bucket is located (S3 destination only).

roleArn.description: ARN of the cross-account IAM role Omni will assume to write to the S3 bucket (S3 destination only).

schedule.description: AWS EventBridge cron expression (minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week year)

showContentLink.description: If true, include a link to the content

showFilters.description: If true, show applied filters in output

slackRecipientType.description: Slack recipient type (Slack destination only). Use "channel" to deliver to a single Slack channel, or "users" to deliver to one or more Slack users via direct message.

testNow.description: If true, run immediately instead of scheduling
